yaqin.title: Yaqīn
yaqin.body: Certain knowledge — the highest grade of epistemic warrant. The classical tradition recognizes three channels: direct sense perception, the first principles of reason, and tawātur (mass-transmitted testimony where collusion is impossible).

zann.title: Ẓann
zann.body: Probable opinion — strong enough to act on, but not strong enough to ground foundational belief.

shakk.title: Shakk
shakk.body: Doubt — when the evidence on each side appears balanced. A weak basis for action; further investigation is called for.

jahl.title: Jahl
jahl.body: Ignorance. A claim at this level cannot anchor a moral decision.

tawatur.title: Tawātur
tawatur.body: Mass-transmitted testimony where collusion or shared error is implausible. A genuine channel of certainty in the classical epistemology.

niyya.title: Niyya
niyya.body: Intention — the inner orientation that re-qualifies an outward act. The hadith says: ‘Actions are only by intentions.’ Two people can perform the same outward act with different intentions; they are doing two different acts in the morally relevant sense.

maqasid.title: Maqāṣid (al-Sharīʿa)
maqasid.body: The objectives the Lawgiver pursues through revealed rulings — preservation of religion, life, intellect, lineage, and property. Al-Shāṭibī orders them across three tiers: ḍarūrī, ḥājī, taḥsīnī.

daruri.title: Ḍarūrī
daruri.body: Necessity — without it, human life collapses structurally. The classical five (religion, life, intellect, lineage, property) belong here. A clear ḍarūrī prohibition overrides most other calculations.

haji.title: Ḥājī
haji.body: Need — not strictly indispensable, but its absence creates significant hardship. The Sharīʿa's licit concessions (rukhṣas) operate at this level.

tahsini.title: Taḥsīnī
tahsini.body: Refinement — the dignity, beauty, and excellence of human life. Preserved as long as the higher tiers are not breached.

haqq.title: Ḥaqq
haqq.body: Truth as reality — a thing being what it presents itself to be. The reality side of Russell's ‘correspondence' relation.

sidq.title: Ṣidq
sidq.body: Truth as speech — the proposition matches reality. The speech side of the same correspondence relation.

ribā.title: Ribā (Usury)
ribā.body: Gain from a loan or from an unequal exchange of like commodities. Forbidden in clear Qur'ānic terms (Q. 2:275 onward). Modern financial application is heavily debated.
